---
title: "Lecture 22: Revision & Mini Project (JavaScript & DOM)"
subtitle: "{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}"
author: "{{< meta instructor.name >}}"
date: "February 26, 2026"
code-annotations: below
---

## {#title-slide data-menu-title="Title Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="custom-title">
[Lecture 22]{.baby-blue-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Revision & Mini Project (JavaScript & DOM)]{.white-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Week 8 | Unit II: JavaScript & DOM<br>
{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}<br>
{{< meta instructor.name >}}, {{< meta instructor.title >}}
</div>

---

## {data-menu-title="Session Goals"}

[Session Goals]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Revise core JavaScript and DOM concepts
- Connect events, validation, and rendering in one workflow
- Build a mini project with clear milestones
- Practice clean coding and debugging strategy
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Revision Map"}

[Revision Map]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A[Variables and Scope] --> B[Functions]
  B --> C[Events]
  C --> D[DOM Selection]
  D --> E[DOM Updates]
  E --> F[Validation]
  F --> G[Mini Project]
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Quick Revision"}

[Quick Revision Checklist]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
- `let` and `const` over `var`
- Reusable functions for repeated logic
- Event-driven UI updates
- `querySelector` / `querySelectorAll`
- `classList` for visual state changes
- Form validation before submit
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Mini Project Brief"}

[Mini Project: Smart Task Tracker]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
Build a single-page task tracker where students can:

- Add tasks
- Mark tasks complete
- Filter by status
- See live count of pending tasks
- Validate empty input before adding
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Project Structure"}

[Project Structure]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.text}
mini-project/
  index.html
  styles.css
  app.js
```

```{.html code-line-numbers="|1-2|4-5|7-9|11"}
<input id="taskInput" type="text" placeholder="Enter task">
<button id="addBtn">Add Task</button>

<div id="stats">
  Pending: <span id="pendingCount">0</span>
</div>

<ul id="taskList"></ul>
<div id="error" class="error-msg"></div>
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Step 1"}

[Step 1: Add Task Logic]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-4|6-11|13-16"}
const taskInput = document.querySelector('#taskInput');
const addBtn = document.querySelector('#addBtn');
const taskList = document.querySelector('#taskList');
const errorBox = document.querySelector('#error');

addBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
  const text = taskInput.value.trim();
  if (!text) {
    errorBox.textContent = 'Task cannot be empty';
    return;
  }

  errorBox.textContent = '';
  appendTask(text);
  taskInput.value = '';
});
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Step 2"}

[Step 2: Render and Complete]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-4|6-13|15"}
function appendTask(text) {
  const li = document.createElement('li');
  li.textContent = text;

  li.addEventListener('click', () => {
    li.classList.toggle('done');
    updatePending();
  });

  taskList.appendChild(li);
  updatePending();
}

function updatePending() { /* next slide */ }
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Step 3"}

[Step 3: Pending Counter]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-3|5-7"}
function updatePending() {
  const tasks = [...document.querySelectorAll('#taskList li')];
  const pending = tasks.filter(li => !li.classList.contains('done')).length;

  document.querySelector('#pendingCount').textContent = pending;
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Enhancements"}

[Suggested Enhancements]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Add delete button for each task
- Filter buttons: All, Pending, Completed
- Save tasks in `localStorage`
- Add due dates and priority tags
- Keyboard shortcut: Enter key adds task
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Debugging"}

[Debugging Plan]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
1. Use `console.log()` for input and event flow
2. Verify selectors are not null
3. Check class toggling and style rules
4. Test edge cases: empty input, duplicate tasks
5. Keep functions small and test incrementally
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Submission"}

[Mini Project Submission Checklist]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Working add/complete flow
- Input validation with clear message
- Live pending count
- Clean code structure (`index.html`, `styles.css`, `app.js`)
- Short README describing features and limitations
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Wrap Up"}

[Wrap Up]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
This revision + mini project session combines:

- JavaScript basics
- DOM manipulation
- Event handling
- Validation and rendering

These are essential foundations before moving into server-side web topics.
:::
